summary: Reviewed the brand event feed changes across API routes, brand persistence, feed adapters, PDF/DOCX integration, and migration assumptions. The main risks are around credential fallback semantics, incomplete update validation, and URL safety assumptions that are weaker than the code comments imply. Overall the adapter shape is sensible, but the secret-handling and activation paths need tightening before merge.
appears_sound: Brand responses expose only `event_feed_has_key` and do not return `event_feed_api_key`, which keeps stored secrets out of normal GET/list responses in `lib/brands/brandRepo.ts:26`.
The generate route fetches full feed credentials server-side via `getBrandFeedConfig` rather than passing API keys through the client in `app/api/generate/route.ts:325`.
The BaronsHub adapter maps provider-specific events into the existing `NormalisedEvent` shape used by PDF and clipboard rendering in `lib/eventFeed/baronshubAdapter.ts:91`.

findings.description: A custom `anchor_management` base URL can be paired with the global `MANAGEMENT_API_TOKEN` fallback when the brand has no stored key. If the anchor adapter sends that key as its API credential, a misconfigured or attacker-controlled HTTPS host could receive the global management token.
findings.evidence: `getBrandFeedConfig` only falls back `baseUrl` when it is missing, but independently falls back `apiKey` to `process.env.MANAGEMENT_API_TOKEN` for any anchor feed with no brand key in `lib/brands/brandRepo.ts:211`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The impact is reduced if only trusted admins can edit brands and custom anchor base URLs are intentionally allowed.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the anchor adapter request headers and decide whether env-token fallback should occur only when the base URL also comes from the env default.

findings.description: Updating a brand cannot clear an existing feed API key, including when the feed is disabled or switched to another provider. This leaves stale credentials in the database and can make `event_feed_has_key` true for an unrelated or inactive feed.
findings.evidence: The PUT route only includes `event_feed_api_key` in `dbInput` when a non-empty key is supplied, so blank input preserves the previous DB value in `app/api/brands/[id]/route.ts:61`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Preserving a blank password field may be intentional for ordinary edits, but there is no separate clear-key path shown in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Update a keyed brand to `event_feed_type: 'none'` and verify whether `event_feed_has_key` remains true or the DB key remains populated.

findings.description: The PUT route does not enforce the POST route's required URL/key rules for non-anchor feeds. Direct API updates can produce a 500 from the DB constraint or persist an empty-string base URL that passes the SQL `IS NOT NULL` check but leaves the feed inactive.
findings.evidence: PUT validates the URL only when `feedUrl` is truthy and checks only key length, while the migration constraint accepts any non-null `event_feed_base_url` and `event_feed_api_key` in `supabase/migrations/20260511120000_add_event_feed_to_brands.sql:17`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The current UI tries to provide URL and key for non-anchor feeds, so normal form usage may avoid part of this path.
findings.what_would_confirm: Send PUT requests that set `event_feed_type: 'baronshub'` with missing or empty URL/key and compare the response/status with POST validation behavior.

findings.description: The URL safety validator claims to reject hostnames that resolve to private or link-local IPs, but it only checks literal hostname strings. This leaves DNS-based private resolution, DNS rebinding, and missing ranges such as IPv6 link-local outside the protection for server-side feed fetches.
findings.evidence: `isPrivateIp` is documented as checking whether a hostname resolves to private ranges, but the implementation only strips brackets and tests string prefixes in `lib/brands/validation.ts:5` and `lib/brands/validation.ts:16`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If feed URLs are restricted to trusted operators, the practical SSRF exposure is lower, but the implementation still contradicts its stated safety model.
findings.what_would_confirm: Add validation tests for a hostname resolving to `127.0.0.1` and for `https://[fe80::1]`, then decide whether to use DNS resolution or a stricter hostname allowlist.

findings.description: The generate route now assumes a resolved brand exists before fetching any upcoming events, so installations without a default brand may silently lose the previous env-based Anchor events. The migration updates existing default brands but does not prove that every database has one.
findings.evidence: The old unconditional `fetchUpcomingEventDetails` call was replaced by `brandConfig ? await getBrandFeedConfig(brandConfig.id) : null` and `feedConfig ? await fetchEventsForBrand(...) : []` in `app/api/generate/route.ts:325`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: There may be a seed or migration outside the pack that guarantees at least one default brand in every environment.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check the seed/migration history or run generation against an environment with no brands to verify whether upcoming events still appear from env configuration.

empty_categories: No evidence that the new public Brand response leaks the raw event feed API key.
No material PDF/DOCX field-shape mismatch found; `NormalisedEvent` preserves the fields the changed consumers reference.
No race-condition issue found in the changed brand defaulting logic beyond the pre-existing non-transactional default update pattern.
